Each verified to bring its partition to 0 validation failures (dry-run). amount_total authoritative; tl+fc=total. NOT for genuine source defects or whole-section format breakage (those stay flagged).

opening_balance = 0 is SOURCED, not derived: the report discloses the prior period as PROSE — '(31 Aralık 2025: Bulunmamaktadır)' — i.e. the bank POSITIVELY states it had no non-performing loans at 31.12.2025. Same precedent as the prose-disclosed Stage-3 credit_quality overrides: a value a table-anchored extractor cannot read, but which the filing states in words. A one-off cell does not justify that risk.

COLENDI's first-ever NPL. The III/IV/V roll-forward IS printed (p49, plain text layer, rendered and coordinate-verified) under "h.2) Information related to non-performing loans:" — a single current-period block, no prior block (the prior IS the opening row). Transcribed from the page, not derived.
THREE independent extractor defects hide it, and fixing only the first would NOT recover it:
  1. _HEADING_RX fails — COLENDI heads the note "Information RELATED TO non-performing loans"; the word "movement" never appears, so gate 1 rejects the page and it is never parsed. (_GROUPS_RX passes.)
  2. THE REAL BLOCKER: COLENDI's get_text() is CELL-PER-LINE — the label and each of the three group values land on separate lines. _THREE_NUMS_TAIL needs `label num num num` on ONE reconstructed line, so it matches ZERO rows even with the heading gate bypassed (verified: rows parsed = 0). Recovering this needs x-coordinate row assembly — the same class as the known loans_by_stage §7.2 column-split gap.
